reasoning: The Philadelphia Eagles won Super Bowl LIX, making them the defending champions for the 2025-2026 season. Initial odds for Super Bowl LX placed them as the favorites or co-favorites at +600 to +700 (implied probability of about 12.5% to 14.3% before vig). Mid-season reports (up to early November 2025) do not reveal significant catastrophic injuries to key players like Jalen Hurts or Saquon Barkley, and the team was widely predicted to perform strongly and contend for the division. While mid-season odds for Super Bowls fluctuate, defending champions performing competitively typically hold odds in the +500 to +800 range through November. Removing standard sportsbook vig (~15-20%), the fair probability of any top-tier favorite at mid-season is generally between 10% and 15%. Given the absence of negative shocks and the expected strength of the roster, 13% is a reasonable and well-calibrated estimate.
